title: About me
desc: I'm Mike. I like building foundation models and pointing them at things -- things like galaxies, stars, the Earth, and hearts. Check out my CV here.

title: The Platonic Universe
desc: Do foundation models see the same sky 🧐? We point models trained on natural imagery at galaxies from major sky surveys -- and find they converge on the same representations, despite never seeing space before. The bigger the model, the more they agree.

title: The Multimodal Universe
desc: 100TB of crossmatched astronomical data from the world's most important telescopes and observatories, packaged up just right for all your deep learning needs.

title: AstroPT
desc: A foundation model for astronomy. Neural scaling laws predict how well it handles downstream tasks -- and the bigger it gets, the better it gets. Open-source, obviously.

title: EarthPT
desc: A foundation model for Earth observation that can forecast the future of any location on the planet. Peek inside its latent space and it'll tell you what matters most.

title: Astronomia ex machina
desc: A review of neural networks in astronomy, where we called the foundation model era before it was cool. Also doubles as my thesis -- hit the mortarboard for the full thing.

title: AstroDDPM
desc: Diffusion models can dream up galaxies that are indistinguishable from the real thing -- physically and visually. They can also zap satellite trails and 'galaxify' any image. Click here to cycle through some fake galaxies, or here for fake APODs.

title: Pix2Prof
desc: What if we captioned galaxies like images? An encoder-decoder model turns galaxy photos into light profile data -- basically teaching a neural net to describe what it sees in the language of astrophysics.

title: SAGAN
desc: A GAN that generates fake astronomical observations so good they fool statisticians. We pushed it to its limits and generated a 7.8 billion pixel synthetic observation of deep space.

title: Neuralnet-MCG
desc: CNNs that diagnose heart attacks from ECG scans and 3D magnetocardiogram video. The model correctly learns that the ST segment is the smoking gun for myocardial infarctions -- no cardiologist needed to tell it that.

title: Heart-CNN
desc: A 3D siamese CNN that spots dead cells in heart tomography scans. Point it at a heart, it finds the damage.
